name: Token and revoke endpoints via vanilla bridge
outcome: POST /api/oauth2/token and POST /api/oauth2/revoke work through the vanilla Fetch bridge, including correct OAuth error responses.
scope: token + revoke route handlers mounted under /api; vanilla bridge in and out; OAuthException error mapping.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/oauth2/token issues access + refresh tokens for a valid authorization_code + PKCE exchange
POST /api/oauth2/revoke returns 200 for a valid revoke request
An OAuthException is mapped to the correct error status/body in the response
steps: src/app/oauth/routes (mounted under /api) — POST /api/oauth2/token: const req = await requestFromVanilla(c.req.raw); return responseToVanilla(await server.respondToAccessTokenRequest(req))

routes — wrap both handlers in try/catch; the vanilla adapter ships no handleExpressError equivalent, so on `OAuthException` (instanceof, imported from @jmondi/oauth2-server) return c.json({ status: e.status, message: e.message, error: e.errorType, error_description: e.errorDescription ?? e.error }, e.status) (mirrors handleExpressError) and re-throw non-OAuth errors
tests — token endpoint: a valid code + PKCE verifier yields access+refresh tokens; an invalid client or verifier yields an OAuth error status
notes: Mirrors the old token/revoke controllers which double-wrapped requestFromExpress and used handleExpressError. With vanilla, requestFromVanilla returns an OAuthRequest directly and there is NO handleVanillaError — error mapping is hand-rolled to match handleExpressError's body shape (verified against @jmondi/oauth2-server v4.0.10). Full happy-path issuance is also covered by the slice-11 flow once authorize exists.
